description: Legal query in the hand of Moshe b. Levi ha-Levi regarding a man who married a widow, then found in her a "hidden defect" from which he contracted an illness. He is a poor man without even enough money for food and has no source of livelihood except public charity. He is in bad straits because whenever he demands a divorce, she insists on receiving her ketubba payment in full. Does the law permit him to pay it in installments? He insists that he is unable to pay one lump sum because of his illness and poverty. On verso there is a fragment of a page of piyyutim in the hand of Moshe b. Levi ha-Levi. ASE.
tags: illness: hidden, illness: marad, widow, Illness: women's, illness: marital, Illness, Moshe b. Levi, illness letter 969-1517, installments, legal query, illness: contagious, illness: poverty, 'ayb, divorce
description_he: שאלה הלכתית על גבר עני שמצא פגם נסתר באשתו האלמנה וחלה, ומבקש לשלם כתובה בתשלומים
